heading: Example
body:
The following index example uses the `sample_mflix.movies` collection
to demonstrate how to configure up to `4` partitions for the data in
the collection. You can use the Visual Editor or the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) Editor in
the Atlas UI and [MongoDB Search indexes](fts-manage-indexes) to create the index.

- Create a file named `indexDef.json` similar to the following:

  ```
  {
      "collectionName": "movies",
      "database": "sample_mflix",
      "definition": {
        "mappings": {
         "dynamic": true
        },
      },
      "name": "partitioned_index",
      "numPartitions": 4
    }
  ```

- Run the following command to create the index.

  ```shell

  atlas deployments search indexes create --file indexDef.json

  ```

```shell
curl --header "Authorization: Bearer {ACCESS-TOKEN}" \
--header "Accept: application/json" \
--header "Content-Type: application/json" \
--include \
--request POST "https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/clusters/{clusterName}/search/indexes" \
--data '
  {
  "collectionName": "movies",
  "database": "sample_mflix",
  "name": "partitioned_index",
  "type": "search",
  "definition": {
    "analyzer": "lucene.standard",
    "mappings": {
      "dynamic": true,
    },
    "numPartitions": 4,
    "searchAnalyzer": "lucene.standard"
  }
}'
```

codeBlocks.content: atlas deployments search indexes create --file indexDef.json
